title: Podcast: Tesla’s disaster earnings, Beijing Auto Show is mad, new EVs galore
content: In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Tesla’s disaster earnings, the Beijing Auto Show being absolutely insane, and a plethora of new EVs unveiled this week.

title: Kia’s EV sales surged 54% in Q1 with a complete lineup from electric sedans to vans
content: Kia set a new global sales record in the first quarter, driven by strong demand for electric vehicles. With a full lineup ranging from the entry-level EV2 to the PV5 van, Kia now offers an EV for nearly everyone.

content:

title: Why the effects on the Iran conflict will be felt on the US grid – This Week in Cleantech
content: On this episode of the This Week in Cleantech podcast, Bianca Giacobone of Latitude Media discusses how Iran's strikes on aluminum smelters in the UAE and Bahrain last month pushed prices up 11% to their highest level since Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

title: Kia cuts EV6 prices by $5,000 on 2026 models
content: Kia’s electric SUV just got a bit more attractive. The 2026 EV6 is considerably cheaper than last year’s model, with up to nearly $5,500 in savings.

title: ABB E-mobility’s new EV fast charger kills peak power hype
content: ABB E-mobility just debuted a new EV fast-charger system that’s trying to change how operators think about building EV charging sites, and it’s not about chasing the highest power numbers anymore.

title: Gas prices got you down? Learn about EVs this weekend at events countrywide
content: It’s that time of year again, time for events across the country to show off electric vehicles at Drive Electric Earth Month. And it couldn’t come at a better time, as gas prices are spiking

title: BYD upgrades one of its top selling EVs with 5-min flash charging and 390 miles range
content: BYD launched its new Flash Charging system, enabling EV charging in as little as 5 minutes, in luxury vehicles like the Denza Z9 GT. Now, it’s set to roll out to some of its best sellers.

title: XPeng unveils GX flagship SUV with 750 km range, L4-ready hardware for $58,000
content:
XPeng officially unveiled the GX, its new flagship SUV, at the Beijing Auto Show today. The full-size six-seater starts at 399,800 yuan (~$58,000) and packs up to 750 km (466 miles) of AWD range in its pure electric version.

The GX represents XPeng’s most ambitious push into the premium market yet, bringing together the company’

title: ABB's New DC Fast Charger Goes Into Megawatt Territory
content: With the right dispenser, ABB Mobility’s OM M-Series power cabinet can deliver up to 1.2 megawatts for an EV.

title: The 2026 Kia EV6 Just Got A Massive $5,000 Price Cut
content: The American-made electric crossover is now cheaper than the Tesla Model Y.

title: The Porsche Cayenne Coupe Electric Trades Space For Style: First Look
content: Porsche's new Cayenne Electric gets a sloped-roof sibling with a few exclusive options. We got an early preview.

title: Li Auto to officially launch L9 Livis flagship SUV with in-house developed chip on May 15
content:
Li L9 Livis is equipped with two in-house developed 5nm M100 smart driving chips, delivering 2,560 TOPS of computing power.

For details, please visit CnEVPost.

title: Smart begins #6 EHD pre-sales in China, its largest model to date
content:
Smart #6 EHD pre-sales in China start at 189,900 yuan ($27,780), with an official launch scheduled for June.

For details, please visit CnEVPost.

title: Saudi Arabia begins qualifying bidders for 3GW/12GWh battery storage RFP
content: Saudi Arabia's state-owned Saudi Power Procurement Company has started the qualification process for its second large-scale build-own-operate BESS tender.

title: Plus Power signs ESA for 800MWh Alabama BESS
content: Developer-operator Plus Power and utility Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) announced a 20-year energy storage agreement (ESA) for the 200MW/800MWh Crawfish Creek battery energy storage system (BESS) in Alabama, US.

title: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: How European energy price volatility is driving the business case for storage
content: How can energy storage capitalise on energy price volatility in European markets, and how do investors need to think about future opportunities and challenges? An expert panel at the Energy Storage Summit 2026 in London in February dived into the topic.

title: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: How much LDES does the UK need, and at what price?
content: How much long-duration energy storage (LDES) does the UK system need, is the government's cap-and-floor the right way to procure it, and what does the long-term picture look like?

title: China’s energy storage battery exports hit 27.3GWh in Q1 2026
content: China exported 84.1GWh of batteries in the first quarter of 2025, of which around one-third was for stationary storage.

title: Grid-forming inverters feature in 74% of Australia’s 33.2GW NEM battery storage pipeline
content: Around 74% of battery storage projects in Australia's NEM pipeline are confirmed to be equipped with grid-forming inverters, says AEMO.

content:

title: Big Ferries Are Becoming Battery-First Systems
content:
Ferries are public infrastructure that happen to float. They are marine buses, freight bridges, medical access routes, school links, tourism arteries, repair crew shuttles, food supply chains, and island lifelines. When they fail, communities notice at once. When fuel costs rise, farepayers and taxpayers notice soon after. That is why ... [continued]

content:

title: AccelerateEU backs 200 GW storage goal but omits dedicated mechanism
content: The European Commission's AccelerateEU energy crisis plan endorses a 200 GW battery storage target for 2030 but proposes no dedicated financing mechanism to reach it, according to SolarPower Europe (SPE), which is calling for a separate EU-wide auction funded by emissions trading revenues.

title: Israeli storage developer moves into solar EPC with majority stake deal
content: Airengy Ltd., listed on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange, has signed a memorandum of understanding to acquire a 51% stake in Green-Go, an Israeli PV developer and engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) contractor with expertise in agrivoltaics.

title: UK solar developer secures $1.1 billion financing package
content: Enviromena announces a £825 million ($1.1 billion) senior portfolio financing package, providing immediate capital to support the buildout of a 1 GW pipeline. The credit facility was underwritten by a group of institutional investors.

title: Developing El Niño points to stronger solar conditions across Australasia and South Asia
content: In a new weekly update for pv magazine, Solcast, a DNV company, provides a June–August 2026 outlook indicating mostly neutral to positive solar irradiance globally, with stronger gains in regions like Australasia, South Asia, West Africa, and Central America—patterns consistent with a developing El Niño. Negative anomalies are limited and mostly early-season, while Europe and the U.S. show modest or mixed signals, with improvements or neutrality dominating as the season progresses.

title: Chinese PV Industry Brief: DKEM seeks cash injection to scale up gigawatt-level production of copper metallization paste
content: DK Electronic Materials (DKEM) plans to raise up to CNY 3.0 billion (USD 413 million) via a private placement to fund PV paste, metal powder expansion, R&D, and debt repayment. The company is advancing high-copper PV paste toward volume production for TOPCon 3.0 cells.

title: Japanese scientists build all-perovskite tandem solar cell with 30.2% efficiency
content: The four-terminal tandem device relies on FAPbI₃ nanoparticles and a spectral splitting design, combining a 24.4% wide-bandgap top cell and a 21.5% narrow-bandgap bottom cell to reach 30.2% efficiency. The system improves light utilization by directing different wavelengths to optimized subcells.

title: Saudi Arabia launches qualification process for 12 GWh of battery storage projects
content: The program comprises six projects with a cumulative power output of 3 GW, each designed to provide four hours of storage duration.

title: Daikin unveils ‘plumb-and-play’ residential heat pump
content: Daikin has launched the Altherma 3 H HT, a pre-engineered air-to-water heat pump using R-32 refrigerant, operating down to −28 C and delivering up to 70 C leaving water temperature for direct boiler replacement in existing radiator systems. The system reportedly achieves a coefficient of performance of 4.86.

title: Inside PV Manufacturing: Midsummer’s CIGS factory in Italy
content: The Swedish thin-film solar specialist produces 50 MW of modules at its Bari facility, using its proprietary DUO system for cell manufacturing. The company says it sources all materials from the EU and other countries in the global North.

title: Bangladesh launches tenders for 77.6 MW of solar
content: Bangladesh’s BPDB has tendered 77.6 MW of solar capacity across three projects in Chittagong, Rangamati, and Dinajpur. The plants will be developed on a turnkey basis with funding from BPDB and the Power Sector Development Fund.

title: How can utilities transform data center backlash into a catalyst for grid modernization?
content: The benefits of data center development to communities in the short and long term are real and quantifiable, but they require a proactive strategy to ensure those who live alongside these facilities feel like partners in their success, rather than spectators to their growth.
